title: [Seminar]Prof. Rajesh K. Balan (Singapore Management University) - ˝The Challenges and Opportunities of Applying Smart Cloud Technologies to Improve IoT Solutions.˝
content: Speaker: Prof. Rajesh K. Balan (SMU) When: 4:30 pm, Nov. 17 (Mon) Where: N1, Room 201 Host: Jun Han Title: The Challenges and Opportunities of Applying Smart Cloud Technologies to Improve IoT Solutions Abstract: My goal in this talk is to inspire students and faculty to work towards solving problems by applying smart Cloud technologies to improve IoT solutions; while still having real-world impact. This type of research is particularly interesting as it requires juggling many different factors; including a) how do you fund this? b) how do you find research-worthy yet possible to deploy problems? c) how do you find appropriate commercial partners for this? d) what are the unique challenges inherent in this type of research, and e) what are the benefits / risks / disadvantages of this type of research? As part of this talk, I will share my own experience of conducting this type of research for the last 15 or so years -- across many different domains such as power management, taxi fleet management, improving indoor spaces, and improving the performance of olympic-level athletes. I will also highlight the excellent opportunities that working with Cloud technologies and IoT allows for doing this type of research. Bio: Prof. Balan is an ACM Distinguished Scientist and has worked in the area of mobile systems for over two decades. He obtained his Ph.D. in Computer Science in 2006 from Carnegie Mellon University under the guidance of Professor Mahadev Satyanarayanan. Over the years, Prof Balan has established a very strong reputation in the mobile systems research community with numerous publications in MobiSys, SenSys, etc. In addition, he is also very active in SIGMOBILE organised events and has served as the program chair for HotMobile 2012 and MobiSys 2019 as well as serving on the Program Committee for UbiComp, SenSys, and MobiSys. He was a co-general chair for both MobiSys 2016 and UbiComp 2018, both of which were held in Singapore and attracted record attendance levels. In addition, he also serves as the ACM SIGMOBILE Student Outreach Director and has conducted numerous student events, called ASSET, as part of this. He is an Associate Editor-in-Chief of IEEE Pervasive and is in the top 2％ lifetime of his field according to the infamous Stanford survey.
